content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission


July 18, 1989


Frances Hong, City Clerk
City of Stockton
425 N. El Dorado Street
Stockton, CA 95202

Re: Combined Campaign Reports
Our File No. A-89-408


Dear Ms. Hong:

This is to confirm advice provided to Nora Keating of your
office regarding the campaign disclosure provisions of the
Political Reform Act.1/ This letter constitutes formal advice
under Section 84205, which states:

The Commission may by regulation or written advice
permit candidates and committees to file campaign
statements combining statements and reports required
to be filed by this title.

The City of Stockton's 1988 primary and general elections,
which have been the subject of litigation, have finally been
scheduled for November 7, 1989, and February 6, 1990. In
addition, the city will conduct another regular primary election
on June 5, 1990. Because the November and February pre-election
filing periods overlap the regular semi-annual and June pre-
election filing periods, we believe it is appropriate to combine
certain of the reports.



_________________
1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Frances Hong
Page 2

     Therefore, persons being voted upon in all three elections
should file campaign disclosure statements on the following
schedule:

Type of Statement               Period Covered          Filing Deadline

1st Pre-Election for           July 1²/ - Sept. 23    September 28
November election

2nd Pre-Election               Sept. 24 - Oct. 21     October 26

Semi-Annual combined           Oct. 22 - Dec. 23      December 28
with 1st Pre-Election
for February election

2nd Pre-Election               Dec. 24 - Jan. 20      January 25

1st Pre-Election for           Jan. 21 - March 17     March 22
June election

2nd Pre-Election               March 18 - May 19      May 24

Semi-Annual                    May 20 - June 30       July 31

     Persons not being voted upon in all three elections will not
use this schedule. Candidates and committees involved in only the
November and/or June elections will use the 1989 Filing Schedule
contained in the 1989 "Information Manual on Campaign Disclosure
Provisions of the Political Reform Act" in connection with the
November election, and the 1990 Standard Filing Schedule which
will be contained in next year's 1990 "Information Manual" in
connection with the June election.

     In addition, please be advised that because all three
elections will occur during a single fiscal year (July 1, 1989
through June 30, 1990), the contribution limitations imposed by
Proposition 73 will apply in the aggregate to contributions
received by candidates running in the elections. Therefore, an
individual may not contribute more than $1,000 in the aggregate to
a single candidate being voted upon in one or all of the
elections. A political committee may not contribute more than


_______________________
²/The period covered by any campaign statement begins the day
after the closing date of the last statement filed. If no
previous statement has been filed during the calendar year, the
period covered by the first pre-election statement should begin
January 1, 1989.

Frances Hong
Page 3

$2,500 to a single candidate, and a broad based political
committe may not contribute more than $5,000 to a single
candidate during the fiscal year.

Please call me at (916) 322-5662 if you have additional
questions.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

[Signature: Carla Wardlow]

By: Carla J. Wardlow
Assistant Chief, Technical
Assistance & Analysis Division
